2024-01-05.summary: Inflation signals moderated while growth data softened across housing and auto sectors, and policy expectations eased. Equities and international growth readings were mixed to lower, with rate and debt concerns emerging.

2024-01-12.summary: Labor data came in stronger than expected, keeping policy expectations hawkish and inflation readings mixed. Equities pulled back while geopolitical tension rose amid shipping-route and regional conflict disruptions.

2024-01-19.summary: Growth signals softened internationally, led by weakness in a major European economy and mixed regional data, while an inflation reading surprised to the upside. Policy expectations stayed dovish and equities eased modestly.

2024-01-26.summary: Equities rallied to fresh records on rate-cut optimism as growth data surprised to the upside domestically and abroad. Geopolitical tension stayed elevated, pressuring shipping costs, while policy easing expectations built globally.

2024-02-02.summary: Policy expectations stayed patient on rate cuts even as growth data came in mixed across regions and inflation continued cooling. Credit risk rose sharply on property-sector defaults abroad, while geopolitical tension eased slightly.

2024-02-09.summary: A blockbuster jobs report reinforced policy patience on rate cuts, with growth data broadly surprising to the upside. Credit risk edged higher on commercial real estate and bank strain, alongside rising fiscal debt concerns.

2024-02-16.summary: A hotter-than-expected inflation print triggered a hawkish repricing of policy expectations and a pullback in equities, while growth data diverged sharply across regions with two economies slipping into contraction. Credit risk rose on a sovereign downgrade tied to conflict.

2024-02-23.summary: Equities extended gains to fresh records on a tech-driven rally, while policy expectations stayed hawkish with no urgency to cut. Inflation signals continued to soften and geopolitical trade tensions were mixed.

2024-03-01.summary: Inflation signals were mixed across housing and insurance costs even as a key gauge moderated. Credit risk rose on property-sector defaults abroad and geopolitical tension increased amid new financial sanctions.

2024-03-08.summary: Inflation sentiment stayed mixed heading into the election cycle while policy expectations remained hawkish across major economies. Credit risk rose on renewed bank losses and regulatory scrutiny tied to regional lenders.

2024-03-15.summary: Inflation ticked up and a strong jobs report reinforced a cautious stance on rate cuts. Credit and geopolitical risks stayed elevated, with bank-sector reflection and continued conflict-related trade tension.

2024-03-22.summary: Policy expectations were mixed as one central bank held steady while another delivered a historic hike, and inflation signals stayed inconclusive. Credit risk rose on fraud allegations abroad and mounting fiscal debt concerns.

2024-03-29.summary: Equities pushed to new highs even as tension persisted between tech strength and policy caution. Growth signals weakened on a major supply-chain disruption, while trade tension stayed mixed amid easing and new friction abroad.

2024-04-05.summary: Equities extended a record-setting run even as inflation readings stayed mixed and above target, reinforcing a patient policy stance. Geopolitical tension eased modestly amid renewed diplomatic economic engagement.

2024-04-12.summary: Inflation rising, policy expectations repriced, output supported. Equities falling, rates elevated, trade tension rising.

2024-04-19.summary: Hawkish policy signals, a sharp equity selloff, and surging borrowing costs coincided with escalating regional conflict following a direct military exchange. Inflation readings stayed elevated and mixed even as underlying growth data surprised higher.

2024-04-26.summary: Growth slowed while inflation stayed sticky, weighing on equities through an extended losing streak. Policy expectations diverged internationally and geopolitical tension stayed elevated amid regional conflict.

2024-05-03.summary: Policy expectations were repriced firmly hawkish as officials cited a lack of inflation progress and rate-cut bets faded. Growth stayed resilient overall despite elevated rates, while credit risk rose on a regional bank failure.

2024-05-10.summary: Policy expectations turned dovish on cooling labor demand, though some coverage kept tightening in play. Growth eased slightly, inflation abroad moderated, and elevated borrowing rates persisted.

2024-05-17.summary: Policy expectations repriced hawkish as confidence in cooling inflation wavered, even as price data showed mild moderation. Equities rallied and growth surprised to the upside, while geopolitical tension around frozen assets simmered.

2024-05-24.summary: Policy caution increased on persistent inflation concerns even as price growth abroad cooled sharply. Equities hit a milestone high, growth signals softened, and geopolitical and trade tensions intensified broadly.

2024-05-31.summary: Growth data was revised lower for the prior quarter while underlying inflation pressures, especially insurance and housing costs, stayed elevated. Geopolitical and trade tension around China and Russia intensified, with early signs of credit strain among smaller lenders.

2024-06-07.summary: Policy turned decisively dovish as a major central bank delivered its first rate cut in years, while inflation readings stayed mixed across regions. Equities saw pronounced volatility around elections and idiosyncratic trading, and growth signals in commercial real estate softened.

2024-06-14.summary: Policy expectations turned hawkish after officials signaled fewer rate cuts despite cooling inflation and strong hiring and wage data. Growth stayed robust, trade tension with China intensified, and rate-sensitive borrowing costs remained elevated.

2024-06-21.summary: Growth signals softened as housing data disappointed and fiscal debt concerns mounted, while inflation continued to moderate. Equities were mixed between a technology-led rally and election-driven weakness abroad, and geopolitical and fiscal tension rose amid political risk in Europe.

2024-06-28.summary: Growth signals softened as supply chains strained and consumer spending weakened, while credit risk was mixed between reassuring bank resilience tests and rising real-estate loan sales. Equities gained on a technology valuation milestone, and geopolitical tension eased modestly on trade talks.

2024-07-05.summary: Inflation continued cooling and policy commentary turned more dovish. Growth was mixed across consumer spending, equities showed underlying volatility beneath calm headline moves, credit risk ticked up in commercial real estate, and trade tension edged higher.

2024-07-12.summary: Policy expectations turned more dovish as inflation cooled further and investors priced in a rate cut, though job growth showed mixed signals. Geopolitical and political tension rose amid election and unrest-driven cost-of-living concerns, with an isolated credit stress episode in fintech lending.

2024-07-19.summary: Policy commentary stayed cautiously dovish without a clear rate-cut signal, while inflation trends were mixed across regions. Growth data disappointed abroad, equities fell sharply on a technology-sector selloff, and trade tension escalated alongside a lingering credit stress episode.

2024-07-26.summary: Growth data surprised meaningfully to the upside even as a major foreign central bank cut rates unexpectedly. Equities fell sharply on a technology-led selloff and rotation into smaller stocks, while credit risk in commercial real estate looked contained and geopolitical tension stayed mixed.

2024-08-02.summary: Policy signals diverged sharply as one major central bank cut rates for the first time in years while another hiked, with inflation continuing to ease domestically. Growth data was mixed between productivity gains and labor-market concerns, and a milestone rise in fiscal debt added to credit risk.

2024-08-09.summary: A growth scare triggered global market turmoil as labor data disappointed sharply, spurring speculation of emergency policy easing. Equities fell steeply worldwide before stabilizing, and falling yields reflected a rapid flight to safety.

2024-08-16.summary: Inflation falling, policy expectations repriced lower, equities mixed after volatility. Output modestly supported.

2024-08-23.summary: Output sharply revised lower, policy expectations dovish. Rates falling, equities modestly supported, trade tension rising.

2024-08-30.summary: Policy expectations turned firmly dovish, inflation and output softening. Equities supported, credit spreads modestly widening abroad.

2024-09-06.summary: Inflation and rates fell while growth and equities softened, with rising volatility and lower policy expectations.

2024-09-13.summary: Inflation and policy expectations fell as central banks cut rates and prices cooled. Growth stayed mixed with softer hiring, and equities were choppy on shifting rate-cut bets.

2024-09-20.summary: Policy expectations repriced sharply lower with a large rate cut and broad global easing. Rates and inflation fell, equities rose to records, and growth expectations were modestly supported.

2024-09-27.summary: Policy expectations continued falling as global central banks cut rates further. Growth was mixed, boosted by a stronger rebound revision but weighed by strike risk, while equities stayed subdued and trade tension edged up.

2024-10-04.summary: Equities rose sharply in China on policy support while Japan slipped, and inflation continued cooling. Growth softened as a major port strike began, adding supply disruption and mild trade tension.

2024-10-11.summary: Labor market data surprised to the upside and a major strike was resolved, while inflation cooled less than expected. Equities rallied on China's policy shift and geopolitical tension rose on oil and tariff risk.

2024-10-18.summary: Growth firmed on stronger retail spending, bank earnings, and a large stimulus pledge abroad, while policy stayed on an easing path. Equities were mixed and geopolitical tension around conflict and oil markets increased.

2024-10-25.summary: Growth softened as foreign data disappointed and a major strike deepened after workers rejected a new contract. Inflation continued easing while geopolitical tension tied to financing support stayed mildly elevated.

2024-11-01.summary: Bond markets and policy expectations repriced sharply on anticipated fiscal and trade risk, highlighted by a steep foreign rate rise, even as inflation kept cooling. Growth was mixed as consumer strength offset strike costs and weak foreign output.

2024-11-08.summary: Equities rose sharply to records and currency strengthened on a decisive election outcome, even as rates rose despite a policy rate cut. Growth was mixed between a weak jobs report and a major strike resolution, while policy uncertainty ticked up.

2024-11-15.summary: Equities extended a rally to their best week in over a year as inflation rose and currency strengthened. Growth was supported by a large foreign stimulus package while trade-related tension increased.

2024-11-22.summary: Equities pulled back as prior rally enthusiasm faded and retail signals turned mixed. Trade-related tension edged higher.

2024-11-29.summary: Inflation accelerated and trade-related tension rose on tariff and currency threats, while foreign growth weakened sharply. Equities gained modestly and retail signals were positive.

geopolitics.narrative: trade tension rising, currency uncertainty elevated

2024-12-06.summary: Trade and currency tension rising, credit spreads widening abroad. Inflation modestly firmer, equities mixed.

2024-12-13.summary: Global policy stance turned decisively dovish, output firming on a strong jobs print. Trade tension rising on tighter supply.

2024-12-20.summary: Policy expectations repriced hawkish as forward rate guidance tightened, triggering an equities selloff. Output remains supported.

2024-12-27.summary: Inflation firmer on preferred Fed measure, equities supported as fiscal tension eased. Output modestly softer amid lingering uncertainty.

2025-01-03.summary: Equities modestly bullish entering the year while trade tension rises across multiple fronts; inflation and growth signals stay soft, with emerging credit risk concerns.

2025-01-10.summary: Labor market and bank earnings pointed to solid growth momentum while yields climbed and Fed policy expectations firmed after a narrow rate-cut decision.

2025-01-17.summary: Inflation accelerated and reinforced firmer Fed rate expectations, pushing yields higher; equities and growth signals turned mixed while trade tension stayed elevated.

2025-01-24.summary: Equities hit records on easing inflation concerns even as tariff threats and trade tension escalated; growth signals abroad stayed mixed.

2025-01-31.summary: Fed policy expectations firmed with rates on hold while global yields climbed and an equities selloff added volatility; growth and inflation stayed mixed amid ongoing trade tension.

2025-02-07.summary: New tariff actions lifted inflation concerns and trade tension while the Fed signaled patience; growth and equities showed mild strain.

2025-02-14.summary: An unexpectedly hot inflation print pushed yields up and firmed Fed rate expectations, while sweeping oversight cuts widened credit risk; growth signals stayed mixed.

2025-02-21.summary: Fed policy firmed and its independence came under new pressure, while trade tension weighed on growth abroad; credit risk signals stayed mixed amid regulatory tug-of-war.

2025-02-28.summary: Equities posted their worst week since inauguration on rising trade tension, while inflation expectations firmed; growth signals softened and credit oversight continued to roll back.

2025-03-07.summary: Equities fell sharply alongside declining yields and rising trade tension, with credit-oversight risk widening as inflation eased and growth signals softened.

2025-03-14.summary: A stock market correction accompanied intensifying growth concerns and a larger-than-expected easing in inflation, while trade tension stayed elevated and Fed policy remained on hold.

2025-03-21.summary: The Fed held rates steady while projecting higher inflation and slower growth, adding to growth concerns; equities stayed choppy near correction levels amid persistent trade tension.

2025-03-28.summary: Equities weakened further on tariff-driven anxiety while trade tension rose sharply amid a notable security lapse; credit risk climbed alongside softer growth signals.

2025-04-04.summary: A sweeping new tariff regime triggered a sharp equities selloff and a surge in trade tension, with inflation risk rising and growth expectations weakening.

2025-04-11.summary: A rare bond-market selloff raised safe-haven doubts even as equities swung sharply on tariff whiplash; growth concerns deepened and trade tension stayed high while Fed rate expectations firmed.

2025-04-18.summary: Fed policy independence came under renewed pressure amid surging inflation expectations, while currency and safe-haven concerns lingered; equities traded choppy and trade tension stayed elevated without fresh escalation.

2025-04-25.summary: Equities rebounded and bond-market tension eased as trade and Fed-independence conflicts showed signs of de-escalation, though global growth forecasts weakened and inflation pressure on households persisted.

growth.narrative: output falling, demand constrained, sentiment subdued

2025-05-02.summary: Growth signals weakened broadly, with falling output and constrained demand across manufacturing and hiring, while trade tension stayed elevated and equities traded mixed with elevated volatility.

fed_policy.narrative: policy stable, rates elevated, expectations revised

2025-05-09.summary: Policy expectations held steady with rate cuts pushed further out, equities recovered strongly, growth readings were mixed, and trade tension eased modestly on new bilateral deals.

2025-05-16.summary: Trade tension eased sharply on a new truce that lifted equities, while fed policy stayed on hold and inflation readings remained subdued ahead of tariff pass-through.

bonds_rates.narrative: rates rising, liquidity constrained, sentiment elevated

2025-05-23.summary: Bond yields and credit risk repriced sharply higher following a sovereign rating downgrade and mounting deficit concerns, while equities traded mixed and growth signals softened on tariff uncertainty.

2025-05-30.summary: Fed policy stayed on hold as trade tension rose again on renewed tariff threats, credit-risk sentiment remained elevated on fiscal concerns, and growth signals softened modestly.

2025-06-06.summary: Inflation stayed subdued as consumer spending slowed, credit risk indicators rose on household debt stress and deficit concerns, and growth signals softened on weak trade data.

2025-06-13.summary: Fed policy remained on hold following a solid jobs report, inflation stayed subdued, and trade tension continued around critical minerals and ongoing bilateral talks while growth signals were mixed.

2025-06-20.summary: Geopolitical tension spiked sharply on an escalating Middle East conflict, driving volatile but resilient equities, while fed policy held steady amid mixed inflation signals and rising credit risk from record debt levels.

2025-06-27.summary: Geopolitical tension spiked sharply on direct conflict before easing into a ceasefire, lifting equities, while fed policy signals turned more dovish as officials voiced support for earlier rate cuts.

2025-07-04.summary: Equities hit record highs even as political pressure on the fed chair intensified, inflation ticked higher, and credit risk rose on a major fiscal bill advancing through the legislature.

2025-07-11.summary: Fed policy signals turned more uncertain amid internal divisions and political pressure over central bank leadership, while trade tension rose on new commodity tariff threats and equities stayed resilient.

2025-07-18.summary: Inflation accelerated on tariff pass-through while fed policy uncertainty spiked sharply amid an intensifying conflict over the central bank chair's future, with equities staying resilient.

2025-07-25.summary: Fed policy tension remained elevated as political pressure on the central bank chair persisted, while equities advanced on a new trade deal and geopolitical trade tension stayed mildly elevated.

2025-08-01.summary: Growth data softened notably in the first half as tariffs weighed, inflation ticked higher, and fed policy stayed on hold despite intensifying political pressure and internal divisions.

2025-08-08.summary: A weak jobs report and the abrupt removal of the top labor statistics official drove fed policy expectations sharply more dovish, while equities fell on renewed growth fears and inflation ticked up from tariffs.

2025-08-15.summary: Inflation accelerated further on tariff pass-through while growth signals weakened broadly through hiring pullbacks and layoffs, fed policy stayed near a high bar for cuts, and trade tension rose on new export controls.

2025-08-22.summary: Fed policy uncertainty escalated sharply as political pressure on the central bank intensified ahead of a key policy speech, while equities continued climbing despite mixed growth and trade tension signals.

fed_policy.narrative: policy repriced, rates falling, uncertainty elevated

equities.narrative: equities rising, sentiment supported, volatility elevated

2025-08-29.summary: Fed policy turned dovish amid an unprecedented independence fight, lifting equities and easing rates; growth was supported by investment spending while trade tension rose on tariff enforcement.

fed_policy.narrative: policy revised, rates falling, uncertainty elevated

2025-09-05.summary: Fed policy remained under political strain as confirmation and independence fights continued, with inflation holding stable. Growth signals were mixed and trade tension eased after a court setback to tariffs.

fed_policy.narrative: policy revised, rates falling, expectations repriced

growth.narrative: output falling, demand subdued, expectations revised

2025-09-12.summary: A sharp downward jobs revision and stalling labor market reinforced the case for rate cuts, even as firmer inflation kept policy cautious. Political strain in Europe added to tension, while equities saw modest support.

fed_policy.narrative: policy revised, rates falling, expectations repriced

2025-09-19.summary: The Fed delivered its first rate cut of the year amid mounting internal division and continued fights over its independence, adding a contested new governor mid-decision. Hiring showed signs of wobbling even as inflation ticked up, while equities stayed supported.

growth.narrative: output falling, demand constrained, uncertainty elevated

geopolitics.narrative: tension rising, trade constrained, uncertainty elevated

2025-09-26.summary: The Fed's rate-cut debate intensified as the new governor pushed for sharper cuts even while Powell called the situation challenging. Geopolitical trade tension rose on energy pressure and supply-chain competition, and tariff uncertainty weighed on growth.

growth.narrative: output falling, expectations constrained, uncertainty elevated

2025-10-03.summary: A government shutdown clouded the economic outlook, straining the Fed's data flow and independence even after a court setback for the administration. Growth signals softened for small businesses under tariff pressure, equities traded mixed, and new tariff announcements lifted trade tension.

fed_policy.narrative: policy revised, rates falling, expectations repriced

2025-10-10.summary: Fed officials grew more divided as hiring slowed and a shutdown-driven data blackout clouded the outlook, reinforcing a dovish lean. Safe-haven demand surged to a record, equities traded mixed and volatile, and political turmoil in Europe added to trade tension.

growth.narrative: output falling, demand subdued, expectations constrained

2025-10-17.summary: Credit stress resurfaced sharply with a bankruptcy scare and comparisons to historic crashes, while renewed tariff threats sent equities tumbling and growth signals softened. The Fed stayed on a dovish path even as some questioned its inflation confidence.

fed_policy.narrative: policy revised, rates falling, expectations mixed

2025-10-24.summary: The Fed delivered another rate cut but signaled doubt about further easing as shutdown-driven data gaps clouded the outlook. Funding-market strain pushed short-term rates higher, equities were lifted by a tech milestone, and a trade framework eased some tension.

fed_policy.narrative: policy revised, rates falling, expectations repriced

growth.narrative: output falling, demand mixed, expectations subdued

2025-10-31.summary: Concerns about a sharper slowdown intensified as a top Fed official and White House aides pressed for faster rate cuts, with growth signals softening despite strong holiday spending forecasts. Credit risk concerns grew around AI financing and consumer debt, while global trade tension stayed elevated.

growth.narrative: output falling, demand subdued, supply mixed

2025-11-07.summary: Fed division deepened while growth signals softened amid a jobs-data blackout and weakening exports and consumer spending abroad. Credit risk concerns grew around AI-related debt and a private-equity bust, and equities stayed cautious on crash comparisons.

2025-11-14.summary: Markets stayed data-starved even after the government reopened, with the Fed easing bank oversight and stock-rally momentum showing signs of strain. Growth concerns grew abroad as a major economy contracted, while affordability messaging underscored persistent inflation pressure.

equities.narrative: equities falling, volatility elevated, sentiment subdued

2025-11-21.summary: Odds of a rate cut rose even as questions grew about the administration's plans for the Fed. Equity markets turned volatile on tech-spending unease and a crypto slide, while fiscal tightening abroad and price sensitivity at home kept growth and inflation concerns in focus.

equities.narrative: equities falling, volatility elevated, sentiment subdued

2025-11-28.summary: Odds of a rate cut rose even as questions grew about the administration's plans for the Fed. Equity markets turned volatile on tech-spending unease and a crypto slide, while fiscal tightening abroad and price sensitivity at home kept growth and inflation concerns in focus.

equities.narrative: equities rising, volatility elevated, sentiment mixed

2025-12-05.summary: Growth signals were mixed as private payrolls softened even as holiday spending held up and political pressure mounted on the incoming Fed pick to deliver cuts. Equities stayed volatile but near records amid a crypto slide, while consumer credit stress and trade tension edged higher.

2025-12-12.summary: The Fed delivered another rate cut but internal divisions and a pending court ruling on its board deepened institutional uncertainty as leadership succession speculation intensified. Equities were choppy near record highs, inflation ticked up modestly, and trade tension rose around currency and surplus dynamics.

growth.narrative: output falling, demand subdued, expectations constrained

2025-12-19.summary: Inflation cooled while unemployment rose, deepening divisions at the Fed and adding uncertainty to an already contested succession process. Growth signals softened further even as fresh data only partially resolved the rate-path debate, and trade friction with a key partner persisted.

growth.narrative: output rising, demand supported, expectations mixed

2025-12-26.summary: Growth data surprised to the upside even as private equity showed signs of credit stress and a widening income divide persisted. Geopolitical tension rose around tanker sanctions enforcement, while a foreign central bank's rate hike underscored a broader tightening pulse into year-end.
